rationale: Gate-forwardness clears at every stage. plan-approve: `mindspec validate plan` exits 0 with only the intentional decomposition-scope-redundancy R=0.10 advisory; `validate spec` exits 0. work_chunks (ids 1-4, all depends_on []) align positionally with 4 `## Bead N` sections; each bead has steps/verify/AC/depends-on. adr_citations {0036,0032,0033} all re-read as Accepted with domains intersecting the impacted set (0036/0032 -> workflow, 0033 -> context-system): no adr-cite-irrelevant, no adr-coverage-missing, and none Proposed so impl-approve raises no coverage/citation warning. The disclosed Impacted-Domains fix is mechanical (colon reposition + attribution notes '-' -> '*'); ParseSpec now yields exactly [workflow, context-system]. complete-gates: divergence reads OWNERSHIP at ownerRef==beadHead (verified in divergence.go), so B1's single commit adding internal/trace/** + .golangci.yml claims makes attribution resolve to workflow at the diffed ref; workflow/architecture.md rides the same diff for the OWNERSHIP self-edit. B2's two-domain change touches workflow/overview.md + context-system/architecture.md in the one commit. Doc targets exist and are distinct across beads. Trace golden is a valid proof: `type Alias Event` strips methods but preserves fields/tags, so Alias-marshal == default-marshal byte-for-byte (struct field order, omitempty on SpecID/DurMs/Tokens/Data identical); populated+zero cases cover both always-present and omitempty fields.
findings: validate plan -> exit 0, sole advisory decomposition-scope-redundancy R=0.10 (intentional disjoint file sets across 4 independent beads); validate spec -> exit 0.

ADR domain/status lines re-read from source: ADR-0036 Accepted (workflow, validation, doc-sync, ownership); ADR-0032 Accepted (validation, adr, lifecycle, workflow); ADR-0033 Accepted (context-system). Each intersects an impacted-domain token; no non-intersecting citation; ADR-0031 correctly NOT cited.

divergence.go ownerRef==beadHead attribution confirms B1's same-commit OWNERSHIP claim covers internal/trace/event.go and .golangci.yml at the diffed ref -> no adr-divergence-unowned. OWNERSHIP.yaml is workflow-self-claimed (line 23), so its edit requires a same-diff workflow doc; plan step 6 supplies workflow/architecture.md.

Per-bead doc targets all exist and are distinct: B1 architecture.md, B2 overview.md + context-system/architecture.md, B3 interfaces.md, B4 runbook.md. B2's two-domain source change correctly rides both docs in one bead commit.

Disclosed formatting-fix commit 9a9359ee verified via git show: only colon placement on the two domain bullets and '-' -> '*' on attribution notes; prose byte-identical -> mechanical, no semantic change.

Trace MarshalJSON removal is genuinely safe: field types (string/float64/int/map) carry no custom marshalers, Alias preserves tags, map keys sort deterministically; the byte-identical golden over populated+zero events is a sufficient proof.

NON-BLOCKING: plan leans on the one-commit-per-bead guardrail for B1's same-diff claim and B2's two-doc touch without restating 'single commit' in the bead steps. Robust regardless, since divergence reads OWNERSHIP at beadHead and diffs base..beadHead -- all changes are present at the tip either way.
